Q: We are organizing the Freshman Ball and we want to offer 2 EC subscriptions as a prize. Are you interested in this collaboration?
A: Thank you very much for the proposal! We are not interested at the moment. Also, organizing contests and giveaways with tickets and invitations is a benefit that we grant strictly to festival partners. Failure to comply with this aspect of the regulation may lead to the cancellation of tickets/invitations, and we don't want that.

Q: If I purchased a ticket from iabilet.ro, is it valid for EC?
A: All tickets purchased from partner sites are valid. As they are not tickets purchased from the EC site, we cannot verify them in our ticketing system, but if you need confirmation, please contact our partners to confirm the validity of your tickets.

Q: Could I purchase a ticket on olx, and not on the EC website?
A: EC tickets can only be purchased from our website or our partner websites. A full list of authorized ticketing partners can be found here: https://electriccastle.com/tickets-info

Q: I entered the wrong name of the person who owns the ticket and I can't edit it now. Can you help me?
A: For small typos, you can write us an email at contact@electriccastle.ro. If more than 48 hours have passed since the ticket was purchased, another person's data can only be transferred to the ticket via the Exchange platform.

Q: I recently changed my ID and I can't edit the data on my ticket, what can I do about this?
A: Hello! Leave us your current ID card details, we will help you with the change in the system.

Q: I lost my ID card a few days ago, how could I access the festival then?
A: You will be able to access the festival area with another legal document that shows your details (name and address), such as a passport/driving license or birth certificate.

Q: What exactly are Youth Pass 21 tickets? Do I qualify if I am 21 years and 8 months old during the festival?
A: Youth Pass 21 tickets are festival subscriptions, at a great price, for all people aged 21 - more precisely, who do not turn 22 by the end of the festival.

Q: I didn't know that children under 7 have free access, so I purchased a Youth Pass for my son. Could I get my money back or buy a tent with that money?
A: Transfer of ticket value for other EC ticket types/offers is not possible, and tickets already purchased are non-refundable, according to the regulations. Please leave us more details, and we will look for a solution in this regard.

Q: I purchased a Youth Pass for my 7-year-old daughter. Then I found out that she gets free entry to the festival. Could I transfer the ticket to a friend of hers?
A: Tickets from the Youth Pass 21 offer are not transferable and cannot be sold through our Exchange platform, according to the regulations: https://electriccastle.ro/youth-pass-21 .

Q: How do I get the free invitation? I am a disabled person.
A: Hello! To obtain the ticket, the eligible person must complete this form: https://www.surveymonkey.com/r/accespersoanecuhandicaptec2025 accompanied by supporting documents.

Q: I understand that I also receive a free invitation for the person accompanying me, is that true?
A: Companions benefit from a free subscription for the 5 days of the festival, General Pass type without camping.

Q: I haven't received the invitation yet. Is everything okay? (invalid document)
A: We have now checked, the document you sent is no longer valid/needs to be revised. We will need an updated document, then we will be able to send you the festival invitation and the discount to the companion.

Q: I haven't received any response regarding my application for Volunteering at EC, is everything okay?
A: Please write to our colleagues at volunteers@electriccastle.ro about this situation. They will be able to help you in this regard.

Q: I want to volunteer at EC, where do I apply? (SOON TO BE OPEN)
A: The official call for volunteers will be opened in the spring, it will be posted on social media pages and on the website. Stay tuned!

Q: I want to volunteer at EC, where do I apply? (ALREADY OPEN)
A: The call for Volunteers is now open. Find more details here: electriccastle.ro/volunteers

Q: I want to volunteer at EC, where do I apply? (CLOSED)
A: The call for Volunteers is currently closed, but we will be able to add you to an additional list. Drop us an email at contact@electriccastle.ro with all the necessary details and we will forward your message to the Volunteer Coordinators. Thanks!

Q: I would need a volunteer certificate after volunteering at Electric Castle, can you help me?
A: You can contact our colleagues from the EC Volunteer team for more details. Here is the email address: volunteers@electriccastle.ro

Q: How far is the festival parking lot?
A: The EC Parking Space is a 5-10 minute walk from the festival entrance gates.

Q: What is the difference between EC parking and Car Camping?
A: The Car Camping area is part of the EC Village, while the EC parking lot is outside this area. The EC parking lot is available for participants who come to the festival by car and who do not want to stay in the EC Village. Details on how to access it will be announced before the festival. In the Car Camping, participants will be able to enter by car and set up their tents in specially arranged areas. You can find the regulations here

Q: I have accommodation in Bontida, how will I be able to park my car?
A: You will need to contact your host in Bontida. They will send the license plate numbers of your car to the City Hall. You will then have access to the main road in Bontida and will have to park in the yard. The police will report cars that are not parked or parked improperly and will apply sanctions.

Q: I will only know on the first day of the festival whether I am coming by car or not, will there still be parking tickets available?
A: We recommend festival-goers to purchase their access tickets to the EC parking space in advance.

Q: Do I have somewhere to leave my bike if I come to the festival by bike?
A: Yes, you can leave your bike tied to the stand near the main entrance to the festival.

Q: Can I come to the festival by motorcycle?
A: We will announce more details about this before the festival.

Q: I would like to receive a tax invoice for my ticket order, is that possible?
A: You can request the tax invoice using the following link, from your EC account: www.electriccastle.ro/request-invoice or by email to our colleagues at financier@electriccastle.ro.

Q: I can't access that link and I need the invoice, what can I do?
A: Please leave an email to our colleagues at financier@electriccastle.ro and they will respond as soon as possible.

Q: My card has expired and I want to update my details to purchase tickets. How do I do this?
A: To replace the card, you need the following: 1. log in to your EC account, 2. go to the My Orders section, 3. click on the “Update credit card” button

Q: I lost my invitation code. What can I do about it?
A: According to the regulation, it is mentioned: In the case of Redeem Codes, their beneficiary, namely the person who received the redeem code, is solely responsible for its storage and use, regardless of whether it comes from Electric Castle or from partners. Losing the code or deleting the confirmation email may lead to the impossibility of use, and all damages arising from this circumstance remain the responsibility of the beneficiary. Electric Castle does not guarantee and is not liable for the loss of the code and the impossibility of its recovery or use.

Q: How could I turn the invitation code into a festival ticket?
A: You can activate the code on your invitation here: electriccastle.ro/redeem-access .

Q: I received an invitation, but I can't make it and I would like to transfer it to a friend, how can I do this?
A: According to EC regulations, invitations, once generated, become nominal and are not transferable, i.e. they cannot be sold or transferred through Exchange.

Q: Do I really have to pay the 50 lei donation for the invitation I have?
A: Guest Pass invitations can only be activated after the mandatory donation for Banffy Castle is paid. After that, the festival ticket will be generated in your EC account.

Q: I lost a pair of glasses at the festival. Can you help me with information?
A: Leave an email to our colleagues at lost@electriccastle.ro, they will help you with an answer in this regard.

Q: How good that you found my glasses! Where can I pick them up?
A: Can you please give us your delivery details? We will send the package by courier / You can pick it up in person in Cluj, at Str.Piezisa, no.19, between 10 am and 6 pm, Monday to Friday.

Q: I paid the first installment for the EC ticket, but I had problems with my bank card. How can I continue paying for my order?
A: For each automatic withdrawal, you received an email notification regarding the deadline for the next installment to be paid. The order is now canceled, and the first installment paid cannot be recovered, according to the installment ticket regulations.

Q: I want to purchase an EC ticket in installments. How can I do that? (EC installment plan unavailable)
A: Currently, the only installment plan available on the site is the one offered through the BT Star card. The installments were available until December 20. Details about the installments will be announced on the site and social media pages.

Q: There's a problem with my card. Can I pay manually?
A: You can use the 'Update credit card details' button and use a new bank card. Manually, you will only be able to pay the remaining amount in full via 'Pay remainder amount'

Q: I want to purchase a White Lodge, but it seems to me that it is out of stock. Are they still available or not?
A: Hey! They appear out of stock, but they are not sold out yet. These are added to the shopping cart of some people. They will receive a notification message with information about the completion of the order. Please check their availability on the site in 24 hours. / These are purchased in installments. Once the installments are successfully completed, the products will become sold out. Otherwise, they will be available again on the site.

Q: I see that the Youth Pass tickets are no longer available. Are they sold out already?
A: We will check with our colleagues immediately and get back to you with an answer. Youth Pass 21 tickets were available in limited numbers.

Q: Hello, I can't log in to my EC account. Can you help me?
A: Send us an email at orders@electriccastle.ro and describe the problem a little. Our colleagues will help you as soon as possible.

Q: I can't remember my EC account password, and the "Forgot my password" button doesn't work. What can I do?
A: We have changed your password now. Please try logging in and then change your password to a new one.

Q: I want to do an internship at Electric Castle. Do you have any positions available?
A: You can find the only available positions here: https://electriccastle.ro/careers We currently have no open internship positions. or The team is currently complete, but you can apply for a project-based role and we will get back to you when we need more people on the team to help us.

Q: Could I work during EC?
A: We currently do not have any positions available within the EC staff. You can apply to be part of the EC Bar team or to be a Cashier.

Q: Hello! I'm a photographer and I would like to be part of the Electric Castle photography team for the next festival edition. How can I join the team?
A: The team of photographers is currently complete. If there is a need to complete it, the colleagues who coordinate the team will contact you. Thank you!

Q: Hello! If we bought return tickets for a certain time, do we have to take that bus or can we take it at another time?
A: Hello! The time on the tickets is not relevant, they can be used at any time for a trip on the EC bus.

Q: Hello. For children under 12 (who have free entry to the festival) what are the conditions for bus transportation?
A: Hello! If they occupy a seat on the bus/train, it is necessary to purchase a regular ticket

Q: Hello! What are the operating hours for the buses? Are they non-stop or do they only leave at a certain time?
A: Hello! When the bus is full, it leaves. It operates non-stop. On Mondays at 12:00 the last bus leaves from Bonțida.

Q: Greetings. If there are still free seats on the train, can they be purchased on site? Because they can no longer be purchased online an hour in advance.
A: Hello! You can only purchase them online. If it helps, there is also the option of taking the bus. They run non-stop

Q: Hello! How long does it take for the EC buses to get to the festival?
A: Hello! The time is generally about half an hour, but during peak times it can reach an hour or even an hour and a half.

Q: Can bus tickets be picked up from the bus at any time?
A: Hello! They can be purchased online and from bus stations where there are dedicated counters. You will be able to pay with your festival bracelet or cash, you will not find POS at the Ticketing points. They cannot be taken from the bus.

Q: Where can I park my motorcycle? Is there dedicated parking? How much does it cost?
A: Hello! You can park in the large parking lot, where cars are also. The entrance is shared, there is no entrance fee and there is only a row dedicated to motorcycles. ***Question examples end***

Contactless MasterCard Bank Card – material element, issued by an accredited bank or institution which constitutes the method of collection/payment of goods sold/purchased inside the Event Venue, according to the Event Regulations.
RFID (RadioFrequency IDentification) Bracelet – the material element that constitutes the only means of collecting/paying for goods sold/purchased inside the Event Venue.
RFID Bracelet Loading – the process by which Event participants will load the "money" payment instrument onto the "RFID Bracelet" payment instrument, a tool with which they will be able to purchase products sold during the Event.
RFID Bracelet Refund – the reverse process of the "RFID Bracelet Recharge" process, namely the process by which Event participants will download the remaining unspent amounts from the "RFID Bracelet" payment instrument to the "money" payment instrument. The RFID Bracelet Refund process requires that the RFID Bracelet Recharge process initially exist.
RFID Bracelet Reader – the device-instrument used to manage the collection/payment of products sold/purchased.
The amount loaded on the RFID Bracelet is a multi-purpose voucher (MPV), as defined by European Commission legislation.
The festival territory is represented by the space in the courtyard and interior of Banffy Castle in Bonţida, Cluj County, where access is allowed to participants who have a valid ticket to the Electric Castle 2025 event, a space delimited according to the signs installed by the Organizer. It does not include the camping area.
The camping area is the accommodation space provided by the Organizer, near the festival area. This area is accessible to participants who have a valid camping pass for the Electric Castle 2025 event. It does not include the festival area.
Indoor area – It is any space that has a roof, ceiling or floor and is bounded by at least two walls, regardless of their nature or their temporary or permanent character.
Outdoor area – Any other space that is not considered indoor.
Website - www.electriccastle.com or www.electriccastle.ro

1. These regulations will also be referred to as Terms and Conditions (“TC”) and apply to the organization and conduct of the Electric Castle event (hereinafter referred to as the Festival), organized by Festival Ticket Management SRL (hereinafter referred to as the Organizer) in the demarcated area of Bonțida commune, Cluj county (hereinafter referred to as the Location).
2. The Regulation contains the provisions that each participant of the event must comply with and their obligations in relation to the Organizer and the other participants in the Festival.
3. These TC can be found on the website, more precisely at the URL: https://electriccastle.ro/terms-and-conditions , and will be displayed at the entrance to the Festival.
4. The Organizer reserves the right to modify these Regulations at any time, without prior notice to the Participant. Any modification of these Regulations will be posted on the event website www.electriccastle.com as well as at the entrance to the event venue, being immediately applicable from the moment of posting.
5. By purchasing a ticket and/or by accessing the Festival, or/and by creating an account on the electriccastle.com website, the Participant declares that he/she has read, understood and undertakes to comply with these Regulations.

1.1. Tickets can be purchased exclusively online, from the website www.electriccastle.com or from the websites of the Organizer's partners, according to the provisions of chapter III.2.
1.2. The organizer reserves the right to change the ticket price without prior notice.
1.3. Participants can opt for several types of tickets and ticket facilities, namely:
General Access Pass (festival pass for the entire duration of the festival)
General Access Pass + Eurolife insurance (festival subscription for the entire duration of the festival)
Premium Access Pass (festival subscription for the entire duration of the festival)
Premium Access Pass + Eurolife insurance (festival subscription for the entire duration of the festival)
Camping Pass (camping pass for the entire duration of the festival)
General Access Pass – This ticket provides access to the festival grounds, excluding the camping and Premium areas. Participants can take part in the event and have the right to stay for the entire duration of the event.
General Access Pass + Eurolife insurance – This ticket provides access to the festival grounds, excluding the camping and Premium areas. Participants can take part in the event and have the right to stay for the entire duration of the event. Eurolife regulations can be consulted here.
Premium Access Pass – This ticket provides access to the festival grounds and the Premium area mentioned on the festival map, for the entire duration of the event.
Premium Access Pass + Eurolife insurance – This ticket provides access to the festival grounds and the Premium area mentioned on the festival map, for the entire duration of the event. Eurolife regulations can be consulted here.
Camping Pass – This ticket provides access to the camping grounds, excluding the festival area. Participants can take part in the event and have the right to stay for the entire duration of the event.
Participants will be able to opt for Camping passes and several types of camping accommodation. These are available here: electriccastle.com/tickets

TO CHANGE THE NAME ON THE TICKET, FOLLOW THE FOLLOWING STEPS:
TICKET HOLDER:
Log in to www.electriccastle.com/my-account/exchange .
Next to the ticket you no longer use, click the "list to marketplace" button, and it will be listed as available on the exchange platform.
If there is someone interested in your ticket, you will receive an email with that person's contact details.
Contact the person to arrange the details of the exchange.
Once you have established and mutually agreed upon the conditions, click the "generate exchange link" button and send the respective link to the interested person.
After the buyer pays the processing fee, you will receive an email confirming the payment has been made.
Go to www.electriccastle.com/my-account and press the “SEND TO BUYER” button.
THE BUYER:
Log in to www.electriccastle.com/my-account/exchange .
Choose the ticket you want and press the "CONTACT SELLER" button - an email will be automatically sent to the ticket holder, who will be informed of your intention.
After you are contacted by the ticket holder, establish and agree on the conditions.
Ask the ticket holder to send you the link and access it.
Fill in the new data that will appear on the ticket.
Pay the processing fee.
The new ticket is generated, but it is still in the account of the original holder, who must complete the exchange operation by pressing the "SEND TO BUYER" button.
Now the ticket is yours and you can download it from your account at www.electriccastle.com/my-account/orders .

1.8. If, following the checks carried out at the entrance or on the territory of the Festival, objects that do not comply with the provisions of the Regulations are identified, they will be confiscated and will not be returned later. Participants have the right to return from the check and leave their respective objects in a space outside the Festival (e.g. car, external accommodation space, etc.) or to throw them in the bins specially arranged for such objects. Exceptions are illegal or illegally possessed objects.
1.9. If illegal objects (e.g. drugs, narcotic substances) or illegally possessed objects (e.g. firearms possessed without a permit) are found on the person, the competent authorities will be notified and the necessary measures will be taken. Identification of such weapons in the Festival territory entails the immediate evacuation of the person concerned.
1.10. Access to the festival premises for minors under the age of 16 is permitted only if they are accompanied by a parent or person exercising parental authority (guardian), who will sign an express declaration assuming responsibility and the obligation to supervise the minor. The Organizer draws the attention of the parents/persons exercising parental authority to the fact that they are responsible for supervising the minor and will be held liable for any damage resulting from failure to comply with this obligation.

In case of suspicion of participants possessing substances that, at first glance, could be classified as psychotropic and narcotic substances, the Organizer has the right to notify the competent authorities in order to verify the medical prescription.
Regarding medications that do not contain psychotropic and narcotic substances, there is no limit, but it is recommended to bring a number of medications that only cover the period of the event.

4.1. Lost objects must be brought by the persons who found them to the Lost & Found area (which will be marked on the Festival map). The owners can recover them by following a procedure of the Organizer which consists of identifying the person and the lost object. The Organizer will keep the recovered objects for a period of no more than 10 days from the moment of handing them over to the Lost & Found area and will try to return them to the owners.
4.2. In the event that the owners of the lost goods are not identified and/or they do not show up to pick them up, they will be handed over to the authorities.
4.3. In the case of perishable or rapidly deteriorating products, the 10-day period may be reduced by the Organizer, without prior notice.

5.1. Inside the Venue, the Organizer ensures security and guarding through a qualified and accredited partner to provide these services. During the Festival, participants are obliged to respect the security agents, to collaborate with them and to follow their instructions in case of emergency.
5.2. In case of incidents of violence or endangerment of the physical integrity of other people, the security personnel will be able to decide to evacuate the people involved from the premises where the Festival is taking place.
5.3. On the territory of the Festival there are first aid areas signposted and indicated on the map, where qualified persons will be active.

6.1. The payment methods for the purchase of services and products on the festival grounds are strictly those made available by the organizers and their partners. As a rule, payment for services and products on the event premises will be made with an RFID (Radio Frequency IDentification) bracelet, but there are partners who also accept cash or contactless Mastercard payments.
6.2. The RFID bracelet can be recharged online via the cashless page or on site, by paying in cash or by bank card at any of the recharge points. The participant can top-up, on site, only a multiple of 10 LEI.
6.3. In the Venue, specially arranged and signposted places are made available to participants for charging RFID bracelets. Charging them entitles the holders to purchase products and services available during the event.
6.4. Unused amounts on the RFID bracelet can be exchanged for money after loading the RFID bracelet, through the process called “Return of unspent amounts on the RFID Bracelet”. The return process is of 2 types:
A. Refunds at the charging points on the festival grounds - the refunded amount represents the entire amount remaining in the RFID Bracelet account, even if online charging transactions were made, the withdrawn amounts will be for all transactions. Throughout the festival, only those who have charged their cash bracelet at one of the charging points on the festival grounds will be able to recover their money each day of the festival.
B. Those who did not recover their money from one of the charging points, but also those who loaded the RFID bracelet via the cashless page or via bank card will still be able to recover the remaining amounts on the RFID bracelet by filling out the refund form, announced in the first week after the end of the festival. More details will be announced at that time. Each participant is obliged to correctly fill in the information and verify the data entered in the form. The Organizer is not responsible if some information (e.g. account details) is not valid. The process of returning the money through the refund form may be subject to bank fees independent of the Organizer.
6.5. Amounts remaining undelivered at the end of the return period from the loading points or manual recovery by completing the online form will become the property of the Organizer.
6.6. During the event, bar and food court products and services are available in specially arranged places, and can only be purchased with an RFID bracelet. All merchants will be obliged to collect the value of the goods sold exclusively with an RFID bracelet.
The tax voucher will only be granted upon purchase of products within the festival.
Participants will not receive a tax receipt for loading funds at the top-up points. These operations will be carried out exclusively for the purpose of crediting the payment account during the festival.

Every ticket (including camping and accommodation tickets) needs to have the holder’s NAME and ADDRESS on it (same as their ID). When adding your details, you will have to upload a PHOTO as well, which won’t be visible on your ticket, but on your EC account only.

Every ticket can be upgraded online or on site.

Minors under 16 can enter if they have approval from a parent or legal guardian, accompanied by them or another adult responsible for supervising them. One adult can accompany up to 3 minors.
Minors aged 16-17 need a parental statement for participation.
The Organizer is not liable for fraudulent statements; the submitter is responsible for their authenticity.
The consent form can be downloaded from here to be filled in. Make sure you have it at the festival's entrance.

To save time, you can visit a Swap Point, show your ID, and swap your ticket into a wristband before the festival begins. Be the first one to brag about it on your Insta stories and avoid the long lines at the entrance of the festival. Pre-Swapped wristbands can go straight to the fast lane.
IMPORTANT: Pre-Swap is not available for day tickets. Minors can only get their wristbands at the festival.
Swap points are available in Cluj-Napoca at Iulius Mall ground floor and in Bucharest at Promenada Mall and AFI Cotroceni.
You can also order your own wristband in Cluj and Bucharest via Wolt, starting from July 8.

Every payment made inside the festival is cashless, as you can pay for anything using your wristband. For that, you need to top-up money on it. In order to do so, you will have to create an account and register your ticket here.
It’s easy, just follow the steps below:
• login or create an account on our website;
• register your ticket code on the Cashless platform;
• top-up money.
This is called an online top-up. When you swap your ticket into a wristband, the wristband will be automatically connected to your cashless account and will have that amount of money on it.
Once registered in the Cashless platform, tickets can no longer be sold through the exchange platform.
On-site top-ups are also available at the festival, every day from 11:00 to 6:00 (the next day).The maximum amount of cash that can be topped up is 2.500 lei.
From 6:00 to 11:00, top-up will be available only online.
Your wristband must be activated inside the festival area to use the top-up amount. After you activate the festival wristband, your balance will be displayed with your top-up. You can activate your wristband by scanning it at a bar or vendor.
After every online top-up, you will have to scan your wristband at any payment point to confirm the new balance.
Refund
IMPORTANT:
Cash refunds of the remaining balance on the wristband, requested at the festival cash desks, are subject to a 3% processing fee. This fee will be deducted from the refunded amount and applies regardless of the method used to load funds onto the wristband.
Refunds requested after the festival has ended, online via bank transfer, are not subject to this processing fee.
By requesting a refund during the festival, the participant acknowledges and agrees to the application of this processing fee.

The Electric Castle festival ground is located 30 km (18 miles) outside Cluj-Napoca, in the village of Bonțida. If you’re not staying in EC Village or Bonțida, you can come by train (EC Trains are the best option), bus, or car.
We’re constantly working on improving transportation to the festival and back.
* A whole street in Bontida will be used to load up to 5 buses at once, making the trip back to town as quick as possible.
* Average 30 min bus queue during peak times.
* Dedicated Whatsapp Chat: For transportation issues only, to stay in touch with you and find solutions ASAP when you need them: +40752653049
Scroll down to get to know all the transportation options.
BY EC TRAINS (INTERREGIONAL)
You can choose to travel to Electric Castle with a dedicated EC train that will leave a few times a day, from Gara Mica (Cluj-Napoca). Only festival goers with valid train tickets and festival tickets will be allowed on this train. The EC train will stop in Jucu, where you will get into a dedicated bus that will take you to the festival. The price of a ticket is 35 LEI and it covers the whole journey Cluj->Bontida.
This is the easiest and fastest way to get to the festival as you’ll avoid the rush hours on road.
Tickets are sold exclusively online. EC trains have limited capacity, so we strongly recommend buying a ticket in advance. When purchasing, select your departure date and time; the ticket will only be valid for that journey.
Unused EC train tickets can be used for any ride with EC buses.
Get your EC Train ticket here

text:
There will be non-stop buses leaving from Iulius Mall and Expo Transilvania to the Castle and back, all day and all night long. We strongly recommend you to buy bus tickets in advance, online (card payment). Tickets will also be available in the bus stations (paying with your wristband, via card, or in cash). A bus ticket costs 35 LEI/way and can be used for any ride during the festival.
Facilities at the Iulius Mall bus station:
24h or 5-day parking lots at Iulius Mall. Go to Info Parking and show your EC wristband to benefit from a discount.
Info Point
Access to toilets in Iulius Mall
Food court and drinks at Iulius Mall
Easy access via CTP buses
First bus from Cluj-Napoca: July 16, 11:00 AM
Last bus from Bontida: July 21, 11:00 AM
Bus stations when returning to Cluj-Napoca: Aurel Vlaicu bus station - near Expo Transilvania, Iulius Mall.
Special rides for EC Village
For those who want to check in early, there will be dedicated buses from Cluj-Napoca on July 14 & 15, leaving from Expo Transilvania, every hour between 11:00-18:00. Tickets can be bought on the spot paying with card or cash (35 lei).

You can also choose to travel to the festival by a regular train. Take the train to Bontida station - any CFR train that passes through Bontida will stop there. You can find the CFR schedule here. Once you get to Bontida, you will have to walk for about 40 minutes until you reach the festival’s entrance.

If you’re traveling from a different country, we recommend you fly to Cluj-Napoca Airport (airportcluj.ro) (30 km from the festival site). From the airport, you can go to one of the departure points of our transport options (Iulius Mall and Expo Transilvania), to the train station, order a ride through , or take a taxi to the festival site.

If you’re traveling from a different country, you can choose to hop on a Flixbus and benefit from a 20% discount on the trip by filling in the form here.
From the bus station, you can go to one of the departure points of our transport options (Iulius Mall and Expo Transilvania), to the train station (10 minutes walk), order a ride through Uber, or take a taxi to the festival site.

We strongly advise you to leave your car at home. Use our trains or shuttle buses!
However, if you choose to come by car, share the ride with your friends. If there are no empty seats when you arrive at the festival, we will offer you free parking (spots are limited) and you get the chance to win some cool prizes. This action is part of our sustainability initiatives to reduce the carbon footprint.
If you are coming to the festival just to drop off / pick up someone, you will have to get a parking ticket and enter the parking area.
Drivers are recommended to take a direct route to the festival from their point of departure. This will help spread the arrivals and departures out and reduce traffic queues.
The chart below shows the peak traffic hours and the times when traffic jams are most likely to occur. Try to avoid arriving at the festival during the red-marked hours, or consider taking the bus or train instead.
The main festival access road is from Rascruci (except for RVs), not Bontida, so make sure you follow the road signs on your way to the castle. If you’re travelling in your RV, you’ll use the main entrance to Bontida through the E576 road. You’ll have to show your RV Ticket at the checkpoint, so have it ready.
Print your Car Camping or RV Camping Ticket and leave it on your car’s board.
If you arrive by car and you’ll be staying in the EC Village, you’ll first need to park your car in the parking area and then take a few minutes walk to the camping area. If you have a Car Camping Ticket, you will enter the designated area from EC Village.
The Parking has a limited number of spots, so we strongly recommend you buy a ticket in advance, online. You will be able to buy Parking Tickets on-site as well. A ticket is available for one entry in the parking area. This means that you can leave your car there for the whole festival period. If you leave the parking, you will have to buy a new parking ticket when getting back. As mentioned above, using the full capacity of your car will give you free parking and the chance to win cool prizes. Carpooling by OMV is part of our sustainability initiatives to reduce the carbon footprint.
There will be a dedicated and limited Premium Parking. In order to have access to it, you will need a parking ticket and you will have to show your Premium wristband or Premium ticket + ID at the entrance.

Uber drivers will have access via E 576 and DJ161. There will be a dedicated pick-up and drop-off spot for Uber and a flat fee of 200 LEI for the trip to the castle.
This flat fee is applied for any ride from Cluj-Napoca to the festival or back.
IMPORTANT: Drivers will only be allowed to the dedicated spot in Bonțida by showing the ongoing ride on the app.

If you choose to come by taxi or other transport app, you will have access via E 576 and DJ161. There will be a dedicated Taxi Station.
IMPORTANT: Drivers will only be allowed there by showing the ongoing ride on the app.

IF YOUR ACCOMMODATION IS IN BONTIDA VILLAGE, IT IS VERY IMPORTANT TO KNOW THIS:
talk to your host regarding the car access and give them the license plate number of the car you will use to get to their address;
your host will have to inform the Auto Accreditation Office about your license plate number;
you will use the main road to Bontida, E576 - DJ161, regardless of what GPS apps instruct you;
upon your arrival in Bontida, head to the Auto Accreditations Office where you will be provided with a printed auto access day pass. This will be valid only for the day of your arrival and only to get to your host's address once;
the cars must be parked inside at all times, for the duration of your stay;
if you leave your host's courtyard by car, when returning, you will only be able to enter the festival’s parking;
cars with no auto access pass will use the road from Rascruci and will head to the festival's parking area.

You can find reusable cups at every bar (except Fresh, Wine, Speed Bars & Coffee Shops). Cocktails or draft beer will be served in them.
For your first drink, you’ll pay a 10 lei deposit for the cup.
For your second order, return the cup to the bar and receive a clean one in exchange.
If you don’t want to carry the empty cup with you until your next order, you can ask the bar for a cup token in exchange for it.
At the end of the festival, you can return the cup/token and get your deposit refunded to your wristband. Returns can be made at the Reusable Cupa point near the Ping Pong stage.
If you paid via wristband: you can get a refund only for the number of cups registered on your wristband, up to 5.
If you paid via card: you can get a refund only once during the festival for up to 5 cups, and the deposit will be refunded to your wristband. A second refund is possible only with proof of transaction.
Cups collected from others will not be accepted.
SAFETY
We want the festival to be a safe place for everyone. You can find our Red Team across the festival, ready to help anytime.
If you ever feel unsafe, call +40741069443 our safety line, or order an “Angel Shot” at the bar. We’ll take care and provide you with extra safety. Behind the Safety Line there will be a team of professional therapists to assist you in case of emergencies.
*These safety tools are here for those who truly need help. We kindly ask you not to use the safety line or the Angel Shot code for non-safety-related situations.
If you have general questions or need festival info, please reach out via email or visit the Info Point inside the festival. Ordering an Angel Shot isn’t a conversation starter with the bartender, it’s a discreet way to ask for help. We count on you to keep these measures available and effective for those who really need support.
Also, if you’d like to rest assured that the drink in your hand is safe, you can ask for a cap and a straw at any bar.
Knowledge is sexy. Visit the www.ginfospot.ro for sex-related advice and resources.
There will be 4 first-aid points inside the festival and one in the EC Village. Check the festival’s map.

Check out the schedule in the EC App.
Check out our sustainability initiatives here and see how you can get involved.
There will be Free Water refill stations inside the festival and EC Village. Bring your own container.

Rules & Regulations
These Rules & regulations apply to the 2026 edition of Electric Castle Festival.
Festival Dates: 16 - 19 July 2026
The organizer of the Festival is SC Electricity Project SRL., based in Cluj-Napoca, Piezișa Street no. 19, room 6, Cluj county, registered at ONRC, no. J12/3193/2024, VAT code 50360413, legally represented by Mr. Mihai Alin PAUN, e-mail: contact@electriccastle.ro.
These Rules and Regulations apply to the users of the website at the time of browsing the website, of purchasing the ticket to participate in the Festival, respectively to the participants from the moment of receiving the ticket of participation until the date of the Festival, as well as during the duration of the Festival.
The Organizer reserves the right to modify the provisions of this Regulation unilaterally, without prior notification, they are immediately applicable from the moment of their publication on the website.
Participants can enter and leave the festival area as many times they want, as long as they have a valid RFID wristband.
Hours of the performances and events are subject to change. Participants cannot claim any damages in relation to such change.
All the participants must pass through a security check at the entrance, in the scope of ensuring the security and integrity of the participants. If they refuse, their access will be denied.
DEFINITIONS
Contactless MasterCard – material element, emitted by a bank or a certified institution, which constitutes a way of collection/payment of goods sold/purchased within the event venue, in conformity with the present TC.
RFID Wristband (RadioFrequency IDentification) – material element that constitutes a way for collection/payment of goods sold/purchased within the event venue.
RFID Wristband Top-up – the process by which participants will change the methods of payment from "cash" to the payment instrument "RFID Wristband” with which they can purchase products sold at the event.
RFID Wristband Money Refund – the inverse process of "RFID Wristband Top-up" and the process by which participants will exchange payment instrument "RFID Wristband" in payment method “cash”. The refunding process of the amount of money implies the initial process of RFID Wristband Top-Up.
The RFID Reader – the device used for the collection/payment of the goods. The amount of money topped up on the RFID Wristband is a multipurpose card voucher (MPV), according to the European Commission Legislation.
The amount loaded on the RFID Wristband is a multi-purpose voucher (MPV), as defined by European Commission legislation.
Festival area – the space marked off by the Organizer consisting of the courtyard surrounding the Banffy Castle and the inside of the Banffy Castle, situated in Bonţida village, Cluj County. In this area the access is allowed only for participants who possess a valid ticket or invite for Electric Castle 2026. This does not include the camping area.
Camping area – the space represented by the accommodation area offered by the Organizer, near the festival territory. Access to this space is permitted for participants who have a valid camping pass for the Electric Castle 2026 event. It does not include the festival area.
Indoor area – any area with a roof or ceiling, separated by at least two walls, no matter their structure or nature, temporary or permanent.
Outdoor area – any area which is not considered indoor.
Website – www.electriccastle.com or www.electriccastle.ro

text:
1. The present Rules and regulations will also be called Terms and Conditions (“TC”) and will apply in regard to the Electric Castle Festival organized by SC Electricity Project SRL (the Organizer) in the designated area in Bonțida, Cluj County (the Location).
2. The present TC contains the rules that each participant has to respect in relation to the organizer and the other participants of the Festival.
3. The TC can be accessed at https://www.electriccastle.com/terms-and-conditions and will be available at the festival entrance.
4. The Organizer has the right to modify the TC whenever, without a preliminary notice of the Participant. Any modification will be displayed on the Organizer’s website www.electriccastle.com and/or at the festival entrance. The modification is of immediate application starting with the moment of publication.
5. By purchasing the ticket, entering the Festival or by creating an account on the electriccastle.com website, the Participant declares that he/she read, understood and obliged himself/herself to respect this TC.

1. TYPES OF TICKETS
1.1. The tickets can be purchased exclusively online from www.electriccastle.com or from our partners websites, according to the terms of chapter III.2.
1.2. The Organizer reserves its right to modify the price of the ticket without preliminary notice.
1.3. The participants can choose between multiple types of tickets:
General Access Pass (festival pass for the whole festival period, without camping)
General Access Pass + insurance by Eurolife (festival pass for the whole festival period, without camping)
Youth Pass U25 (festival pass for the whole festival period, without camping)
VIP Pass (festival pass for the whole festival period, without camping)
VIP Pass+ insurance by Eurolife (festival pass for the whole festival period, without camping)
ULTRA VIP Pass  (festival pass for the whole festival period, without camping)
Camping Pass (camping pass for whole festival period)
General Access Pass – This ticket offers access in the festival area, excluding the camping areas. The participants can attend the event for the whole event period, except for the Premium area or other special access perimeters.
General Access Pass + insurance by Eurolife – This ticket offers access in the festival area, excluding the camping and premium areas or other special access perimeters. The participants can attend the event for the whole event period. The Eurolife regulations can be consulted here.
Premium Pass – This ticket offers access in the festival area and in the Premium area, mentioned on the festival map for the whole event period. Does not include camping.
Premium Pass + insurance by Eurolife – This ticket offers access in the festival area and in the Premium area, mentioned on the festival map for the whole event period. Does not include camping. The Eurolife regulations can be consulted here.
Camping Pass – This ticket offers access in the camping area, open between July 15–19. Does not include access inside the festival area.
* Participants interested in purchasing tickets for Electric Castle have the option to register to be notified when the next batch of tickets becomes available. Registration is done by completing the dedicated form on electriccastle.ro/tickets, where users provide their first name, last name, email address, and phone number. By filling out the form, users consent to receiving email and SMS notifications regarding ticket availability for Electric Castle. Personal data is processed in accordance with the Privacy Policy.
Participants who purchase such tickets declare that they comply with the stipulated age conditions and agree that they will not be given access to the Festival if at ticket validation it is found that they do not meet the conditions. Costs of festival passes and facilities can be found at electriccastle.ro/tickets.
Participants have the option to purchase Camping Passes and tickets for several types of accommodation structures that are announced on electriccastle.ro/tickets.
Types of accommodation:

! The cost of the facilities can be paid exclusively at the time of making the reservation on the website, for the entire period of the festival. For exchanging RON for Electric Castle products, the conversion rate is 1 EUR = 5.10 RON.
* In addition, as part of the "Bonțida Fever" campaign held in the summer of 2023, the Organizer offered free Lifetime Pass tickets to participants who had a permanent black "Bonțida Fever" tattoo and communicated its proof to the Organizer until 26.06.2023. Henna tattoos, semi-permanent tattoos, stickers or any temporary drawings/graphics/paintings are not accepted. To the extent that the Organizer has a reasonable suspicion that the beneficiaries of the Lifetime Pass tickets have defrauded/not respected the previously presented eligibility conditions, in order to obtain the tickets in bad faith, the Organizer reserves the right to ask the beneficiaries, for access to the Festival and/or at the time of picking up the RFID wristband, presenting a current proof of the "Bonțida Fever" permanent tattoo. To the extent that the ticket holders refuse to present proof, the Organizer reserves the right to cancel the ticket with immediate effect (including for future editions), being denied access to the Festival based on the Lifetime Pass ticket. Lifetime Pass participants can upgrade to Premium Access by paying the upgrade amount for the final price of the festival tickets, displayed on the electriccastle.ro website.
1.4. Children under the age of 7 or including, have free access for the whole festival period, with the condition of being accompanied by their parents/guardians that own a valid pass/ticket.
1.5 – Access of people with disabilities to the Electric Castle festival
1.5.1. People with disabilities classified under a degree of disability, as well as their companions, benefit from the following facilities, within the limit of 200 subscriptions for the 4 days of the festival (a limit imposed considering the total capacity of the Festival, the capacity of the accessible areas, as well as safety and security regulations):
Children (minors under 18 years of age) with disabilities, regardless of the degree of disability (severe, accentuated, medium, or mild), and one companion – benefit free of charge from a subscription for the 4 days of the festival of the General Access Pass type (without camping).
Adults with severe or accentuated disabilities and one companion – benefit free of charge from a subscription for the 4 days of the festival of the General Access Pass type (without camping).
Considering that these facilities can only be granted within the limit of a maximum number of 200 places, taking into account the total capacity of the Festival, the capacity of the accessible areas, as well as safety and security regulations, free access will be granted based on the order in which requests are received.
If the number of 200 requests that meet the conditions specified in these Regulations is reached, the Organizer will indicate in these Regulations that the maximum number of subscriptions granted free of charge to people with disabilities has been reached, and from that moment on, the Organizer will no longer respond to requests addressed for this purpose.
1.5.2. Adults with medium or mild disabilities benefit from a 50% discount on the price of the General Access Pass (without camping). The companion of an adult with a medium or mild disability pays the full price. By exception, if the need for a companion has been established by an evaluation commission and can be proven by a document issued by a competent authority or has been recorded in the disability degree certificate, the companion of an adult with a medium or mild disability will also benefit from the same discount as the adult with a medium or mild disability whom they accompany.
1.5.3. Conditions for granting the facilities mentioned in points 1.5.1 and 1.5.2:
The facilities are granted based on the following valid documents, presented for verification:
Disability degree certificate (issued by competent authorities);
Identity document;
Optional, the European Disability Card.
1.5.4. Procedure for granting the facilities mentioned in points 1.5.1 and 1.5.2:
In order to benefit from the facilities mentioned in points 1.5.1 – 1.5.2, adults with disabilities (or, in the case of minors, their legal representatives) must submit a request expressing their desire to participate in the festival and provide proof of fulfillment of the conditions provided in points 1.5.1 – 1.5.3. Requests must be submitted by completing an online form available here, exclusively until 1 June 2026. All requests submitted after 1 June 2026 will not be taken into consideration.
Free or discounted subscriptions are issued only after verification of the documents indicated in point 1.5.3 and subject to fulfillment of the conditions provided in points 1.5.1 – 1.5.3.
The Organizers reserve the right to refuse access in case of presentation of invalid documents or attempts at fraud.
1.5.5. People classified under a severe or accentuated degree of disability undertake the obligation to always attend the Festival accompanied, for reasons of personal safety.
For any questions or additional clarifications regarding the facilities offered according to section 1.5 of the Regulations, please contact the Organizer at contact@electriccastle.ro.
1.5.6. For people with disabilities classified under a degree of disability and their companions, the Organizer will ensure reserved parking spaces, marked with the international symbol, within a minimum limit of 4% of the total parking spaces (but no fewer than 2 spaces).
People with disabilities classified under a degree of disability who hold a parking card for free parking issued under the conditions of Law no. 448/2006 regarding the protection and promotion of the rights of people with disabilities will benefit from free parking, within the limit of available parking spaces.
The parking card must be displayed on the front of the vehicle transporting the person with a disability, so that the front of the card is visible for verification.
1.6. The Organizer will not be held responsible for any incident or accident involving minors or persons with disabilities. All responsibility for them rests with minors/persons with disabilities and/or parents/legal representative or companion. The Organizer draws the attention of parents/legal representatives or persons exercising parental authority to the fact that they have the obligation to supervise the minor and will be held liable for any damage resulting from failure to comply with this obligation.
1.7. The organizer only guarantees for the validity of the tickets that were purchased from Electric Castle or from its authorized partners.
1.8. A complete list with the authorised partners can be found on www.electriccastle.ro.
IMPORTANT!
1.9. Tickets can only be sold by participants exclusively using the Exchange Platform. It is strictly forbidden to sell invites by any means, online or offline. Failure to comply with these provisions will result in the cancellation of the marketed tickets and banning the access to the festival of the persons involved.
1.10. Changing the information of the tickets can be done only by using the Exchange Platform. You can fill in the details for free only once during the first 7 days after purchase. Afterwards, changing the information of the bought tickets can be done in the conditions mentioned at art. III.3.
1.11. Posting the unique series number of the tickets or redeem codes on social media or other websites (e.g Facebook) is strictly prohibited.
1.12. Posting announcements about selling/donating/exchanging tickets on social media, other websites or public spaces is strictly prohibited.
1.13. If the Organizer will identify any counterfeit/forged tickets/invites/wristbands, it will inform the police.
1.14. Purchased tickets cannot be replaced and their countervalue cannot be refunded by any other means except using the Exchange Platform, according to art III.3.
1.15. Purchased tickets are exclusively for personal use. As an exception, purchased tickets can be used commercially, for promo campaigns, giveaways, and contests only with the prior written consent of the Organizer. Failure to comply will result in the cancellation of the tickets used for commercial purposes and the persons responsible will be held accountable for the damage created.
1.16. In the case of Redeem Codes, their holder, respectively the person who received the redeem code, is solely responsible for its storage and use, regardless of receiving it from Electric Castle or other partners. The loss of the code or deletion of the confirmation email can lead to the impossibility of use, and all damages resulting from this circumstance remain the responsibility of the holder. Electric Castle does not guarantee and is not responsible for the loss of code and the impossibility of recovering or using it. The invites are divided in three: Corporate Pass, Partner Pass and Guest Pass. A mandatory donation for the Banffy Castle is charged for the Guest Pass.
2. PURCHASING TICKETS
2.1. Tickets can be purchased exclusively online, using the website www.electriccastle.com, our partners’ websites or by using Electric Castle mobile app (available for iOS and Android, using the online payment system iPay. The tickets can be purchased only by credit/debit card and the ticket will be issued only after the payment will be confirmed by iPay. Afterwards, the participants will receive the ticket in electronic format, in their account on the Website. The tickets are nominal. A buyer can buy up to 6 tickets per account, indicating the name of the beneficiary of each ticket. The buyer can fill in the details for free only once during the first 7 days after purchase. After 7 days the identification data can be changed for a fee only  through the Exchange Platform that can be found at www.electriccastle.com
Tickets purchased on www.electriccastle.com have a unique code assigned at the time of purchase. Selling the ticket or changing the data through the Exchange Platform will invalidate the ticket and a new unique ticket code will be generated.
2.1.1. Personalization Fee Calendar
Participants can personalize their tickets only once for free, within the first 7 days after purchasing them. After this period, the personalization can still be done, but a fee will apply as follows:
Until May 31 2026: €30
June and July 2026: €50
2.2. The purchasing of tickets from the authorized partners will be made according to their terms and conditions. Authorized partners are entitled to charge a management commission, which shall be added to the normal price of the ticket.
2.3. Certain EC Partners offer discount-vouchers or group discounts that can be validated in an online account, created on www.electriccastle.com in the Redeem Code section, according to the terms and conditions and the date of validity, written on the vouchers.
3. THE EXCHANGE PLATFORM
For unforeseen situations, when the owner of the ticket cannot attend the Festival and the name written on the ticket cannot be changed, the owner can use the Exchange Platform to transfer the ticket.
A processing fee of 30 EUR will apply for requests submitted until May 31, and 50 EUR for those submitted after this date.
FULL DETAILS REGARDING THE STEP BY STEP PROCEDURE WILL BE ANNOUNCED IN MARCH 2026.
